CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-19-2023 Set For Trial On Feb-26-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Jerry Dratler'S Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Plaintiffs' Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances.

Defendant Jerry Dratler'S Motion And Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Plaintiffs' Complaint.

The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's "special motion to strike (anti-SLAPP) plaintiffs' complaint" is denied. A cause of action for invasion of privacy is pled. The complaint says defendant photographed private areas of plaintiffs' home from a neighbor's property without permission from anyone. It is also pled that one of defendant's photos included a bedroom window of plaintiffs' minor children and that he "had been researching the location of [the] children's bedroom." Defendant used one photo of plaintiffs' home in an unsuccessful "discretionary review application" he filed with the city planning commission to oppose expansion of plaintiffs' garage. Code of Civil Procedure 425.16 sets "a two-step process for determining whether an action is a SLAPP. First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the challenged cause of action is one arising from protected activity. If the court finds that such a showing has been made, it must then determine whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Navellier v. Sletten (2002) 29 Cal.4th 82, 88.) Here, defendant has not made a threshold showing that the cause of action for invasion of privacy "is one arising from protected activity." (Id.) Rather, the cause arises from illicit photography and research. Speech or petitioning activity - e.g., the discretionary review application - is not the wrong complained of. That defendant used one illicit photograph in the application is "just evidence" of his liability for invasion of privacy. (Park v. Board of Trustees of California State University (2017) 2 Cal.5th 1057, 1060.) The court not having found the threshold showing, CCP 425.16's second step need not be taken. (See Navellier, 29 Cal.4th at 88.)

Judge: Richard B, Ulmer Jr., Clerk: R. Woods, Not Reported. (302/RBU)***After hearing: an order is signed. (See scanned order for details)

Defendant Jerry Dratler'S Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Plaintiffs' Complaint

Atty. REBECCA COLL, Esq. of Quadra & Coll, LLP (Tel: 415-426-3502) for plaintiffs;

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT JERRY DRATLER'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's "motion to strike punitive damanges [sic] from plaintiff's complaint" is denied. For the pleading stage, the complaint adequately states bases for punitive damages. (Id. at 2:13-3:9; Civ. Code 3294.)

Order signed.
